name: fixpoint-common

metadata.name: fixpoint-common
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.license: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Fixpoint common code - an AI that can research the web

documentation:
# Fixpoint common code

Code shared across Fixpoint client and server packages.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing
documentation: Types for parsing web pages and other data sources into an LLM-ready format.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.CreateWebpageParseRequest
documentation:
Request to parse a single webpage.

Parses a webpage and returns the text (non-chunked) of the page. The output
format is markdown.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.CreateBatchWebpageParseRequest
documentation: Request to parse a batch of webpages.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.WebpageParseResult
documentation:
A parse result from a single webpage.

Contains the text (non-chunked) of the page. The output format is markdown.
Parse results are called `...ParseResult` instead of `...Parse` because if we
have a plain `CreateParseRequest`, returning a `Parse` object is confusing
about whether that is a verb or a noun.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.BatchWebpageParseResult
documentation: A parse result from a batch of webpages.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.Chunk
documentation: A chunk of text or media bytes.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.CreateCrawlUrlParseRequest
documentation:
Request to start a crawl parse.

Crawls webpages starting at a URL. Returns the text (non-chunked) per page.
The output format is markdown.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.types.parsing.CrawlUrlParseResult
documentation:
A parse result from crawling a URL.

Contains the text (non-chunked) per page. The output format is markdown.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._shared.CallCacheKind
documentation: Kind of call cache to use

classes.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._shared.CacheResult
documentation:
The result of a cache check

The result of a cache check. If there is a cache hit, `found is True`, and
`result` is of type `T`. If there is a cache miss, `found is False`, and
`result` is `None`.

Note that `T` can also be `None` even if there is a cache hit, so don't rely
on checking `cache_result.result is None`. Check `cache_result.found`.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._decorator
documentation: Decorators for caching functions.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._decorator.cacheit
documentation:
Decorate a sync function for call durability.

Decorate a sync function for call durability, so that we store the results
of calling a function. If the workflow fails and we recall the function, we
can check if we already computed its results.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._decorator.org_cacheit
documentation:
Decorate a sync function for call durability.

Decorate a sync function for call durability, so that we store the results
of calling a function. If the workflow fails and we recall the function, we
can check if we already computed its results.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._decorator.async_cacheit
documentation:
Decorate an async function for call durability.

Decorate an async function for call durability, so that we store the results
of calling a function. If the workflow fails and we recall the function, we
can check if we already computed its results.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._decorator.org_async_cacheit
documentation:
Decorate an async function for call durability.

Decorate an async function for call durability, so that we store the results
of calling a function. If the workflow fails and we recall the function, we
can check if we already computed its results.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._disk._BaseDiskCallCache.check_cache
documentation:
Get the cached result, if it exists.

Check the cache for the cached result for the given run, task/step, and
arguments. If it exists, return the cached result. Otherwise, return
None.

If you provide a `type_hint`, we will load the cached result into that
type. We can load in Pydantic models and dataclasses.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured.errors.StructuredException
documentation: The base class for all structured workflow exceptions.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured.errors.DefinitionError
documentation:
Raised when there is an error in the definition of a structured workflow

The DefinitionException is raised when defining an invalid structured
workflow, task, or step. It can also be raised when we begin executing an
ill-defined workflow, task, or step. It does not mean that computation
raised an exception, but rather that the structure of your workflow program
is wrong.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.utils.retry
documentation: Utilities for retrying operations

functions.name: fixpoint_common.utils.retry.async_retry
documentation:
Decorator for asynchronous retry with jitter and optional exception handling.

Args:
    max_retries: Maximum number of retry attempts.
    base_delay: Base delay between retries in seconds.
    jitter: Maximum random jitter to add to the delay.
    exception_handler: Optional function to handle exceptions. Return True
        if we should re-raise the exception, aborting the retry. Return
        False if we should follow our normal retry logic. Arguments are the
        exception and the current retry count.

Returns:
    A decorator function.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.utils.retry.sync_retry
documentation:
Decorator for synchronous retry with jitter and optional exception handling.

Args:
    max_retries: Maximum number of retry attempts.
    base_delay: Base delay between retries in seconds.
    jitter: Maximum random jitter to add to the delay.
    exception_handler: Optional function to handle exceptions. Return True
        if we should re-raise the exception, aborting the retry. Return
        False if we should follow our normal retry logic. Arguments are the
        exception and the current retry count.

Returns:
    A decorator function.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._helpers
documentation: Internal helpers for the structured workflow system

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._helpers.validate_func_has_context_arg
documentation:
Validate that a function has a WorkflowContext as its first argument

If the function is a method, we expect the first argument to be "self" and
the next argument to be a a WorkflowContext.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._helpers.decorate_with_cache
documentation:
Decorate a task or a step for call durability.

Decorate a step or a task for call durability, so that we store the results
of calling a task or a step. If the workflow fails and we recall the task or
step, we can check if we already computed its results.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._helpers._pull_ctx_arg
documentation:
Return the WorkflowContext from function params, along with its position.

Return the WorkflowContext argument and its position in the args, which will
either be 0 or 1. If the function is a method, we can skip the first
argument because it will be `self`.

Raises a CallException if the WorkflowContext is not found.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._errors
documentation: Internal errors for structured workflows.

classes.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._errors.InternalExecutionError
documentation:
Indicate an execution error, without full workflow context.

When we raise an execution error inside our code, we might not have access
to all of the info about the running workflow. We don't want to have to pass
that info around anywhere we might raise an ExecutionError, so we have an
internal-only version of that error that doesn't require any extra info.

The InternalExecutionError should be transformed into an ExecutionError
before being returned to the user.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._converter
documentation: Converting between deserialized and serialized data formats

functions.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._converter.value_to_type
documentation:
Convert a given value to the given type hint.

This is used internally to convert a raw JSON loaded value to a specific
type hint.

Args:

: hint: Type hint to convert the value to.
  value: Raw value (e.g. primitive, dict, or list) to convert from.
  custom_converters: Set of custom converters to try before doing default

  > conversion. Converters are tried in order and the first value that
  > is not {py:attr}`JSONTypeConverter.Unhandled` will be returned from
  > this function instead of doing default behavior.

Returns:

: Converted value.

Raises:

: TypeError: Unable to convert to the given hint.
pylint: disable=too-many-branches,too-many-statements,too-many-locals

classes.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._converter.JSONTypeConverter

methods.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._converter.JSONTypeConverter.to_typed_value
documentation:
Convert the given value to a type based on the given hint.

Args:

: hint: Type hint to use to help in converting the value.
  value: Value as returned by {py:func}`json.loads`. Usually a scalar,

  > list, or dict.

Returns:

: The converted value or {py:attr}`Unhandled` if this converter does
  not handle this situation.

class_variables.name: fixpoint_common.callcache._converter.JSONTypeConverter.Unhandled
documentation:
Sentinel value that must be used as the result of
{py:meth}`to_typed_value` to say the given type is not handled by this
converter.

Converter for converting an object from Python {py:func}`json.loads`
result (e.g. scalar, list, or dict) to a known type.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._task
documentation:
Structured workflows: task definitions and task entrypoints

In a structured workflow, a task is a section of a workflow. Its state is
checkpointed, so if the task fails you can resume without losing computed work.
In a workflow, agents are able to recall memories, documents, and forms from
past or current tasks within the workflow.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._task.task
documentation:
Decorate a function to mark it as a task definition

A task definition is a function that represents a section (task) in a
workflow. The function must have at least one argument, which is the
WorkflowContext.

An example:

```
@structured.task(id="my-task")
def my_task(ctx: WorkflowContext, args: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    ...
```

documentation: Get the internal task Fixpoint attribute for a function

documentation: The internal Fixpoint attribute for a task function

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured
documentation:
Structured workflows

Structured workflows are a sequence of tasks and steps, where you can use one or
more AIs to get some workflow done. All tasks and steps are "durable", meaning
that their state is checkpointed and can be resumed from. Your LLM agents have
memories about the previous tasks and steps in the workflow, and can save and
load forms and documents that they are processing as part of the workflow.

You can think of Fixpoint structured workflows kind of like Temporal workflows,
but supercharged with the extra features that LLM systems need, like memory,
RAG, and tools for parsing structured output from unstructured documents.

documentation:
Get the workflow run, but only after we have finished the async workflow

This workflow run is returned as an awaitable that only resolves once
the workflow has finished running. For the safety of the workflow, we
don't want code outside a running workflow to have access to the
WorkflowRun object until the workflow run is finished.

documentation:
Whether the workflow is running, or open but waiting before resuming.

A workflow is open when it is actively running, or if it is suspended
while waiting for some other event, such as a human-in-the-loop review.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow_run_handle.WorkflowRunHandle.is_closed
documentation:
Whether the workflow is closed (aka not open).

A workflow is closed when it either succeeds, errors out, is cancelled,
or otherwise stops.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._step
documentation:
Structured workflows: step definitions

In a structured workflow, a step is the smallest unit of work in a workflow.
Each step is checkpointed, so if the step fails you can resume without losing
computed work.

You can call steps from the workflow, or from tasks, but not from other steps.
Within a workflow, the step returns control to the task or workflow after being
called, which can then coordinate the next step or task in the workflow.

In a workflow, agents are able to recall memories, documents, and forms from
past or current steps and tasks.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._step.step
documentation:
Decorate a function to mark it as a step definition

A step definition is a function that represents a step in a workflow. The
function must have at least one argument, which is the WorkflowContext.

An example:

```
@structured.step(id="my-step")
def my_step(ctx: WorkflowContext, args: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    ...
```

documentation: Get the internal step Fixpoint attribute for a function

documentation: The workflow run context's logger

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._context.WorkflowContext.clone
documentation: Clones the workflow context

A context for a structured workflow

A WorkflowContext tracks the current WorkflowRun, and it contains a few
things:

- The `workflow_run` itself, with which you can inspect the current node
  state (what task and step are we in?), store and search documents scoped to
  the workflow, and fill out structured forms scoped to the workflow.
- A logger that is scoped to the lifetime of the `WorkflowRun`.
- The `run_config`, that defines settings for the worflow run. You rarely
  need to access this.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.constants

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows._workflow
documentation:
The workflow and workflow run code.

A workflow is a collection of tasks and steps that are executed in a sequence.
A workflow run is a running instance of a workflow.

modules.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow
documentation:
Structured workflows: workflow definitions and workflow entrypoints

In a structured workflow, a workflow is the highest level of the structured
workflow program. It can call other tasks and steps. Within the workflow's tasks
and steps, we checkpoint progress so if any part fails we can resume without
losing computed work or spending extra on LLM inference.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.workflow
fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.workflow_entrypoint
documentation:
Mark the entrypoint function of a workflow class definition

When you have a workflow class definition, you must have exactly one class
method marked with `@workflow_entrypoint()`. This function is an instance
method, and must accept at least a WorkflowContext argument as its first
argument. You can have additional arguments beyond that.

We recommend that you use one single extra argument, which should be
JSON-serializable. This makes it easy to add and remove fields to that
argument for backwards/forwards compatibilty.

here is an example entrypoint definition inside a workflow class:

```
@structured.workflow(id="my-workflow")
class Workflow:
    @structured.workflow_entrypoint()
    def run(self, ctx: WorkflowContext, args: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        ...
```

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.get_workflow_entrypoint_fixp
documentation:
Get the internal fixpoint attribute for a workflow entrypoint.

Must be called on the entrypoint function.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.get_workflow_definition_meta_fixp
documentation: Get the internal fixpoint attribute for a workflow definition.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.get_workflow_instance_fixp
documentation: Get the internal fixpoint attribute for a workflow instance.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.spawn_workflow
fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.respawn_workflow
documentation: Retries spawning a structured workflow.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.run_workflow
documentation:
Runs a structured workflow, returning its result.

Runs a structured workflow and directly returns its result. This is a
shortcut for `spawn_workflow(...).result()`.

functions.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.structured._workflow.retry_workflow
documentation:
Retries running a structured workflow.

If your workflow run fails, you can retry running that particular run. It
will only run previously failed workflow tasks and steps.

This is a shortcut for `respawn_workflow(...).result()`.

run with that run_id yet, this effectively acts as an idempotency key.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow.WorkflowRun.goto_task
documentation:
Transition to the given task.

Tasks do not need to be declared ahead of time. When you go to a task,
we infer its existence.
pylint: disable=unused-argument

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow.WorkflowRun.goto_step
documentation:
Transition to the given step.

Steps do not need to be declared ahead of time. When you go to a step,
we infer its existence.
pylint: disable=unused-argument

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow.WorkflowRun.clone
documentation: Clones the workflow run

A workflow run.

The workflow run has a cache for objects, such as documents, forms, and
ChatCompletions.

It also has memory to recall previous workflow steps, tasks, and LLM
inferences.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Documents.get
documentation:
Get a document from the cache.

Gets the latest version of the document.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Documents.store
documentation:
Store a document in the cache.

If a document with that id already exists in the workflow, we will throw an
error.

The optional `path` is a "/" separate path of the form "/{task}/{step}".
The "{step}" portion is optional. If you only specify the leading "/",
it is stored at the root of the workflow, outside of all tasks and
steps. By default, we store the document at the current task and step.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Documents.update
documentation: Update a document in the cache.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Documents.list
documentation:
List all documents in the cache.

The optional `path` is a "/" separate path of the form "/{task}/{step}".
The "{step}" portion is optional. If you only specify the leading "/",
we list all documents at the root of the workflow, outside of all tasks and
steps.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Forms.get
documentation:
Get a form from the cache.

Gets the latest version of the form.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Forms.store
documentation:
Store a form in the workflow run.

If a form with that id already exists in the workflow run, we will throw
an error.

The optional `path` is a "/" separate path of the form "/{task}/{step}".
The "{step}" portion is optional. If you only specify the leading "/",
it is stored at the root of the workflow run, outside of all tasks and
steps. By default, we store the form at the current task and step.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Forms.update
documentation:
Update a form in the workflow run.

Updates a form, setting the specified fields. If a field is not preset,
it is not set. To set a field to None, specify it.

methods.name: fixpoint_common.workflows.imperative.workflow._Forms.list
documentation:
List all forms in the cache.

The optional `path` is a "/" separate path of the form "/{task}/{step}".
The "{step}" portion is optional. If you only specify the leading "/",
we list all forms at the root of the workflow run, outside of all tasks
and steps.
